title: Paramiflos glandulosus

The Rutaceae family includes a diverse range of plants, many of which are aromatic and produce fragrant flowers or fruits. The Paramiflos genus is relatively small, but it is part of the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Paramiflos glandulosus is native to certain regions in South America, where it grows in a variety of habitats. This plant's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Rutaceae family.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including the ability to reduce anxiety and insomnia, and to alleviate digestive issues and skin irritations, making it a valuable natural remedy for overall health and wellness.
